title: Creating & Managing Jobs
lede: Jobs are the heart of iMash Dispatch — every service call your business runs lives here, from the first booking to the final payment. This guide walks you A-to-Z through creating a job, reading the job detail screen, understanding every status, and closing a job out with money in the bank.

steps.title: Open the Dispatch Dashboard
steps.content:
The Dispatch Dashboard is mission control for your field work — every job, its status, its money, and its customer, all on one screen that updates live.

  1. In the top navigation, click Dispatch, then click Dispatch Dashboard in the slider below it (or go directly to /pipeline/jobs?tab=dispatch).
  2. Along the top you'll find the date-range controls: a preset picker (for example Recently Updated), a Start date → End date range, and a toggle that decides which date the range filters by — Added Date, Scheduled Start, or Scheduled End.
  3. Below that, live counter cards show Jobs, Draft, In Progress, Completed, Urgent, and Available Techs for the selected range.
  4. The jobs table lists every job with its Job number, Status, Priority, Numbers (the customer's phone), Finance, Client Info (name and service address), Quote, and Actions.
  5. In the Actions column: the green eye (View Details) opens the job, and the grey eye (Hide from dashboard) hides the row without deleting anything — finance stays untouched.
  6. Click Filters to slice by technician, status, job type, or job source.
💡 The same Dispatch slider also holds Kanban View (drag jobs between status columns), Map (jobs and techs on a live map), Messages, and the configuration tabs — this guide focuses on the Dispatch Dashboard and the job screen itself.

images.alt: Dispatch Dashboard with counter cards, status tabs and the jobs table
images.caption: The Dispatch Dashboard — date range, live counters, status tabs, and every job in one table

images.alt: Dispatch Dashboard with counter cards, status tabs and the jobs table
images.caption: The Dispatch Dashboard — date range, live counters, status tabs, and every job in one table

steps.title: Understand job statuses
steps.content:
Every job moves through a simple lifecycle, and the tab strip above the table lets you jump straight to any group. Here's what each status means:

  • Draft — the job exists but isn't confirmed yet. Nothing is dispatched.
  • Scheduled — the job has a date and time and is waiting for its window.
  • Dispatched — a technician has been notified (by SMS or call) and the job is on its way to being worked.
  • In Progress — the technician started the work.
  • On Hold — paused; a technician may still be attached but work is stopped.
  • Completed — work done and paid. Completed jobs lock their finance numbers.
  • Cancelled — called off; kept for reporting.
  • No Answer — the customer couldn't be reached.
  • Unassigned — jobs with no technician yet (the orange badge counts them so nothing slips).
  • Urgent — a priority view of jobs marked high/urgent/emergency.
  • Spam — junk requests you flagged; excluded from your numbers.
💡 The tabs show live counts — an Unassigned badge that isn't zero is your to-do list: those jobs need a technician now.

images.alt: Status tab strip with All Jobs, Draft, In Progress, Completed, Scheduled, Dispatched, On Hold, Cancelled, Unassigned, Urgent and Spam
images.caption: The status tabs — one click filters the table to any lifecycle stage

images.alt: Status tab strip with All Jobs, Draft, In Progress, Completed, Scheduled, Dispatched, On Hold, Cancelled, Unassigned, Urgent and Spam
images.caption: The status tabs — one click filters the table to any lifecycle stage

steps.title: Create a job — customer & basics
steps.content:
Jobs create themselves when your AI agent books a call or a customer submits an online booking form — but you can also create one by hand in under a minute.

  1. Click Create Job (top-right of the Dispatch Dashboard). The Create New Job form opens.
  2. Enter a Job Title — the only required field (for example AC Repair & Tune-Up).
  3. Add a Description so the technician knows exactly what they're walking into.
  4. Pick a Job Type and Job Source — these drive reporting, technician matching, and per-source settings (set them up under Job Sources & Types).
  5. Set the Priority: Low, Normal, High, Urgent, or Emergency.
  6. Choose a Status — new jobs default to Draft; pick Scheduled if it's already confirmed.
  7. Optionally Assign Technician right away, or leave it empty and let Auto Dispatch find the tech for you.
  8. Link the customer: pick an Associated Deal, an Account, Contacts, or Leads — each has a + button to create a new record on the spot.
  9. Under Phone Numbers, click Add to attach the customer's number with a label — this is the number dispatch texts and masked calls use.
⚠️ If a very similar open job already exists (same phone or address), iMash warns you about a possible duplicate before saving — you can open the existing job or choose to create anyway.

images.alt: Create New Job form with Job Title, Description, Job Type, Job Source, Priority, Status, technician and customer fields
images.caption: The Create New Job form — only Job Title is required; everything else enriches dispatch and reporting

images.alt: Create New Job form with Job Title, Description, Job Type, Job Source, Priority, Status, technician and customer fields
images.caption: The Create New Job form — only Job Title is required; everything else enriches dispatch and reporting

steps.title: Add the address, schedule & pricing
steps.content:
Scroll down the same form for the where, when, and how much:

  1. Service Address — fill in Street Address, City, State, and Postal Code. This address shows on the dashboard, the technician's phone, the map view, and invoices.
  2. Scheduling — pick Scheduled Start and Scheduled End, or type Manual Durations (Min) (for example 60) to describe how long the visit should take.
  3. Pricing — enter the Quoted Amount you gave the customer; the full price breakdown is built later on the job's Financials tab.
  4. Special Instructions — gate codes, parking notes, "beware of dog" — anything the technician must know. These are included in the dispatch SMS.
  5. Tags — free-form labels for filtering and reporting.
  6. Click Create Job in the top-right. You'll land back on the dashboard with your new job in the list.

images.alt: Service Address, Scheduling, Pricing, Special Instructions and Tags sections of the Create New Job form
images.caption: The lower half of the form — address, schedule window, quoted amount, and tech instructions

images.alt: Service Address, Scheduling, Pricing, Special Instructions and Tags sections of the Create New Job form
images.caption: The lower half of the form — address, schedule window, quoted amount, and tech instructions

steps.title: Open a job — the job detail screen
steps.content:
Click a job number (or the green eye in Actions) and the full job screen opens on top of the dashboard. The top strip alone tells you the whole story:

  • Status & priority pills — top-left, always visible.
  • Quick actions — Log Activity writes a timestamped note to the job's activity log; Start moves a scheduled job to In Progress; Complete appears once work is running; Spam flags junk; Cancel calls the job off.
  • The status stepper — Draft → Scheduled → Dispatched → In Progress → Pending Payment → Completed shows exactly where the job stands at a glance.
  • KPI cards — Amount, Technician Cut, Company Cut, Commission, and Assigned To (with a one-click assign button when the job is unassigned).
  • Job Info card — the editable Job Title, Job Type, Job Source, Tags, Description, and the customer block with their phone numbers. Edits save automatically.

Below the stepper runs the tab bar — Overview, Notes, Financials, Dispatch, Photos, Related Jobs, SMS / WhatsApp, Email, Quotes, and Invoices — the next steps walk through each one.

images.alt: Job detail screen with status pills, quick actions, status stepper, KPI cards and Job Info
images.caption: The job screen — stepper, quick actions, money KPIs, and the Job Info card

images.alt: Job detail screen with status pills, quick actions, status stepper, KPI cards and Job Info
images.caption: The job screen — stepper, quick actions, money KPIs, and the Job Info card

steps.title: Manage scheduling & status
steps.content:
Still on the Overview tab, scroll to the Scheduling & Status card — the job's control panel for time and state:

  • Status — change the job's status directly from the dropdown (the stepper and dashboard update instantly).
  • Priority — bump a job to High, Urgent, or Emergency so it surfaces in the Urgent tab.
  • Duration Source — Use Scheduled bills time by the booked window; Use Actual uses the real start/end times the technician logged.
  • Manual Durations (Min) — a fixed duration when you'd rather not track clock time.
  • Scheduled Start / Scheduled End — reschedule right here; the calendar and dispatch reminders follow along.
  • Below the fields, a read-only recap shows Scheduled Duration, Actual Start, Actual End, Actual Duration, Job Added Date, and Job Closed Date.
💡 Rescheduling is also one click from the dashboard — the calendar icon on each row opens the same reschedule picker without opening the job.

images.alt: Scheduling & Status card with Status, Priority, Duration Source, Manual Durations and scheduled window fields
images.caption: Scheduling & Status — change status, priority, and the schedule window in place

images.alt: Scheduling & Status card with Status, Priority, Duration Source, Manual Durations and scheduled window fields
images.caption: Scheduling & Status — change status, priority, and the schedule window in place

steps.title: The Financials tab — where the money lives
steps.content:
Open the Financials tab and iMash does the math for you — who pays what, who earns what, and what your business keeps:

  • Commission Override — override the assigned technician's default commission (Percent, Fixed, Per Job, or Per Hour) for this job only.
  • Team Commissions — add cuts for extra team members who helped on the job.
  • Invoicing — click Create Invoice to bill the job (parts, services, tax, card fees, tips), or View All to see existing invoices.
  • Financial Summary — three live cards: Customer (what the customer pays you), Technician (what the tech earns — and what you owe them), and Company (what your business keeps). Flip between Summary and Detailed, and hit Recalculate after changes.

Until an invoice exists the summary shows a Draft preview; the moment you create the invoice, the preview becomes the job's real finance record.

💡 Every number here flows into Finance & Team Reports automatically — commissions, company cut, tips, and parts are never a spreadsheet job again.

images.alt: Financials tab with Commission Override, Team Commissions, Invoicing and the Customer/Technician/Company summary cards
images.caption: Financials — invoicing plus the live Customer / Technician / Company money breakdown

images.alt: Financials tab with Commission Override, Team Commissions, Invoicing and the Customer/Technician/Company summary cards
images.caption: Financials — invoicing plus the live Customer / Technician / Company money breakdown

steps.title: The Dispatch tab — technician history & controls
steps.content:
The Dispatch tab is the job's dispatch diary. Once technicians get involved you'll see, in chronological order:

  • Every dispatch SMS sent to each technician, their ACK (accept) or PASS replies, and reassignments.
  • A per-technician timeline — who was offered the job, who took it, who backed out, and when.
  • Live dispatch timers while an auto-dispatch run is active (time left for a tech to acknowledge, escalation countdowns, and reminder calls).
  • The Dispatch Controls card (shown when Auto Dispatch is enabled) — Restart Auto Dispatch, dispatch to manual picks, Broadcast / Race (blast every eligible tech at once — first to ACK wins), and Stop Auto Dispatch.

On a brand-new job the tab simply says No technician dispatch history recorded yet — it fills up the moment dispatch starts.

💡 The whole SMS-dispatch engine — sequential vs broadcast, escalation, masked tech↔customer calls — has its own guide: Auto Tech Dispatch.

images.alt: Dispatch tab of a new job showing the empty dispatch history state
images.caption: The Dispatch tab — this fills with SMS offers, ACK/PASS replies, and controls once dispatch runs

images.alt: Dispatch tab of a new job showing the empty dispatch history state
images.caption: The Dispatch tab — this fills with SMS offers, ACK/PASS replies, and controls once dispatch runs

steps.title: Photos, notes & every conversation
steps.content:
The remaining tabs keep the job's full paper trail in one place:

  • Photos — click Upload photos or Take photo; technicians also attach before/after shots from their mobile dashboard.
  • Notes — a running, timestamped journal for the office team.
  • Related Jobs — link return visits and follow-ups to the original job.
  • SMS / WhatsApp — every text thread with the customer and the technicians about this job, with a composer to reply right there.
  • Email — emails exchanged with the customer's contact records.
  • Quotes and Invoices — the pipeline documents attached to this job; create new ones (Create Invoice / Assign Existing) without leaving the screen.
  • Call Logs — appears when calls are linked to the job: recordings, transcripts, and AI summaries of the booking call and any masked tech↔customer calls.

images.alt: Photos tab with Upload photos and Take photo buttons
images.caption: Photos — upload from the office, or let techs attach shots from the field

images.alt: Photos tab with Upload photos and Take photo buttons
images.caption: Photos — upload from the office, or let techs attach shots from the field

steps.title: Complete the job — and get paid
steps.content:
Closing out is where iMash protects your revenue: a job can't be marked complete until the money side is real.

  1. When work begins, click Start (or let the technician start it from their dashboard) — the job moves to In Progress.
  2. When the work is done, click Complete in the job header.
  3. If the job has no invoice yet, iMash stops you with a friendly popup: create one from the Financials tab first.
  4. If invoices exist but have an outstanding balance, collect payment — in person (card, cash, tap-to-pay), or by sending the customer a Pay Link by SMS or email.
  5. The moment the last invoice is fully paid, the job flips to Completed automatically — commissions, company cut, and tips lock in, and every report updates on its own.
⚠️ Completed jobs freeze their finance numbers. If something was missed (a late part cost, a correction), reopen the conversation on the Financials tab rather than deleting anything — post-completion cost entries keep your books straight.

That's the full loop: booked by your AI, dispatched by SMS, worked in the field, paid and reported — without a single sticky note.
